perturbation.original_text: 1.1 “Proprietary Information” means any and all information and material disclosed by Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives in connection with the Proposed Transaction or in the course of the parties’ evaluation and negotiation of the Proposed Transaction, together with all communications, data, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, records, notes, lists, financial statements or other materials or information prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that contain or otherwise reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party or that reflect the review of, interest in, or evaluation of all or any portion of the Proposed Transaction or Disclosing Party’s business (collectively, “ Derived Information ”), whether tangible or intangible, furnished or prepared in writing or in oral, graphic, electronic or any other form or manner.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 “Proprietary Information” means any and all information and material disclosed by Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives in connection with the Proposed Transaction or in the course of the parties’ evaluation and negotiation of the Proposed Transaction, together with all communications, data, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, records, notes, lists, financial statements or other materials or information prepared by Receiving Party’s Staff that contain or otherwise reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party or that reflect the review of, interest in, or evaluation of all or any portion of the Proposed Transaction or Disclosing Party’s business (collectively, “ Derived Information ”), whether tangible or intangible, furnished or prepared in writing or in oral, graphic, electronic or any other form or manner.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Representatives' to 'Receiving Party’s Staff', this creates an in-text contradiction because 'Representatives' is a defined term in Section 1.2. The contract later relies on the original definition of Representative to define their actions, but now Representative is not the main party responsibile for any proprietary information, instead the Receiving Party's staff, creating an contradiction when referring to who is responsible for what.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal, and shall cause its Representatives not to disclose or reveal, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information to any person, other than to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Proprietary Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and who are bound by restrictions regarding the disclosure and use of such Proprietary Information (either contractual, legal or fiduciary) owed to Company, Interested Party or any their respective Representatives that are comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement. Each party shall inform all of its respective Representatives and shall cause its respective Representatives to inform their Representatives who receive Proprietary Information hereunder of the confidential nature of such information and the Proposed Transaction, as well as the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, and shall cause its Representatives to not, use any Proprietary Information for any purpose other than to evaluate the Proposed Transaction or in connection with the consummation of the Proposed Transaction. Each party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Representatives' broadly, while the changed text narrows it to 'Receiving Party’s Staff'. The contradicted text places obligations on the 'Receiving Party' and 'Representatives'. By narrowing the definition of 'Representatives' in Section 1.1, the Receiving Party's staff is now specifically responsible for proprietary information, but the Representatives, as defined in 1.2, are still included to handle this proprietary information in the contradicted text. This creates a contradiction because the initial definition has been narrowed, but the responsibilities associated with the original definition remain, causing confusion about who is truly responsible for safeguarding the information.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 “Representatives” means as to any person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, accountants and their respective Representatives).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 “Associated Personnel” means as to any person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, accountants and their respective Representatives).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Associated Personnel' creates an in-text contradiction because the text then uses the original definition of 'Representatives', thus creating confusion. It also contradicts in the fact that the definition is still using Representatives. This means, is the definition for Associated Personnel, the definition of Representatives?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal, and shall cause its Representatives not to disclose or reveal, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information to any person, other than to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Proprietary Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and who are bound by restrictions regarding the disclosure and use of such Proprietary Information (either contractual, legal or fiduciary) owed to Company, Interested Party or any their respective Representatives that are comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement. Each party shall inform all of its respective Representatives and shall cause its respective Representatives to inform their Representatives who receive Proprietary Information hereunder of the confidential nature of such information and the Proposed Transaction, as well as the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, and shall cause its Representatives to not, use any Proprietary Information for any purpose other than to evaluate the Proposed Transaction or in connection with the consummation of the Proposed Transaction. Each party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Associated Personnel' in the definition 1.2 creates an in-text contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text'. The Contradicted Text repeatedly refers to 'Representatives' and their obligations regarding Proprietary Information. By changing the defined term, but not changing the occurrences of 'Representatives' in the rest of the document, it creates ambiguity and contradiction. Is the definition for Associated Personnel, the definition of Representatives? The original definition 1.2 clearly defined 'Representatives'. The changed definition now introduces 'Associated Personnel' but still defines it with Representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its subsidiaries will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'affiliates' to 'subsidiaries', it creates an in-text contradiction because affiliate has a broader meaning than subsidiaries. Subsidiaries is a company that is controlled by another company. Affiliate is more broad term that means 'officially attached or connected to an organization'. Changing it, means affiliate isn't included here anymore, thus contradiction with the intention to have affiliate included
perturbation.contradicted_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its subsidiaries will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction. Additionally, the above restriction shall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party from making offers of employment or offering retention packages to any employees of Company or any of its affiliates in connection with the Proposed Transaction, provided that such offers have been disclosed to the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'affiliates' to 'subsidiaries' in the Changed Text creates a strong in-text contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The explanation states that 'affiliate' is a broader term than 'subsidiaries.' Thus, by narrowing the scope to only 'subsidiaries' in the Changed Text, it implies that 'affiliates' are no longer included in the restriction, which directly contradicts the original intention of the clause and the Contradicted Text, where 'affiliates' are explicitly included in the restrictions. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about the obligations of the Interested Party regarding the employment or solicitation of individuals connected to the Company or its affiliates, as described in both excerpts. The change directly violates the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, as it alters the scope of a key term leading to conflicting obligations.
